scope.summary: Implement auto_seal_panel operation that detects open endpoints in a Panel EdgeShape outline and closes them with a minimal bridging segment or arc, emitting the corrected outline via CRDT
acceptance_criteria: auto_seal_panel(panel_id) detects all open endpoints (distance > eps_mm between adjacent edge endpoints) and inserts bridging Line EdgeShape segments to close them
PANEL_CLOSED ValidationDescriptor passes after auto_seal on any panel with gaps <= 5 cm (panels with larger gaps are flagged as advisory rather than sealed)
The operation is idempotent: calling auto_seal on an already-closed panel is a no-op
Emits TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded with the inserted segment diff
Model-accessible as a post-processing step in the author_garment MCP tool (auto_seal: true flag)
scope.risk_if_missed: Without Auto Seal, every model-authored or sketch-generated panel that has a small gap (due to floating-point coordinate mismatch at sketch endpoints or LLM numerical error) will fail PANEL_CLOSED and be rejected at validation. Models generating GarmentSpec JSON are likely to produce near-closed outlines with sub-mm gaps; without Auto Seal the self-correction loop would require exact coordinate repairs instead of a seal pass.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
